---
title: "Natural deduction and sequent proofs"
page-layout: article
format:
  html:
    include-before-body: _lfl-nav.html
---

For a document on bussproofs for Gentzen-style proofs, two Fitch-style packages, and also mentioning Lemmon style proofs, see Alex Kocurek's document [Proofs in LaTeX](http://www.actual.world/resources/tex/doc/Proofs.pdf).

**Natural deduction and sequent proofs, Gentzen-style**

- The standard package in recent years has been [bussproofs.sty](http://math.ucsd.edu/%7Esbuss/ResearchWeb/bussproofs/).  Note that there is a LaTeX for Logicians [User Guide to bussproofs.sty](pdfs/BussGuide2.pdf). A [bussproofs-extra](https://ctan.org/pkg/bussproofs-extra?lang=en) add-on by Richard Zach enables you to add vertical or diagonal lines of dots for "missing" parts of proofs. 

- Bussproofs is old, has some limitations, but is stable. Some now prefer the following similar but more flexible package: [ebproof](https://www.ctan.org/pkg/ebproof?lang=en).

- And there is another package, which is designed to be even more flexible, unnecessarily so for most uses: [prftree](http://ctan.org/pkg/prftree).

- If you like to cancel discharged premisses by slashing through a wff, the  [cancel](https://www.ctan.org/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/cancel?lang=en)  package is for you. Example of it in use [here](http://tex.stackexchange.com/questions/280103/can-i-typeset-a-proof-tree-in-exercise-2-4-3c-from-mathematical-logic-by-ian-c).


**Natural deduction, Fitch-style**

- The original version of fitch.sty by Johan Klüwer is described in Kocurek's document linked above. But there is now a better later version by Peter Selinger (maintained by Richard Zach), producing similar output: [fitch.sty](https://ctan.org/pkg/fitch).

- [lplfitch](https://www.ctan.org/pkg/lplfitch?lang=en) is a package for typesetting Fitch-style proofs a la *Language, Proof, and Logic*, the logic textbook by Jon Barwise and John Etchemendy. The documentation is good and contains a section comparing its output to the varieties of fitch.sty. (See [here](https://tex.stackexchange.com/questions/239183/changing-the-logic-symbols-in-lplfitch-sty) for how to change the pre-set suite of logic symbols.)

- There is also a package [natded.sty](https://www.ctan.org/pkg/natded?lang=en)  for Jaśkowski-style and Kalish-Montague-style natural deduction (similar to Fitch-style, but with boxes around subproofs).

- [logicproof](https://www.ctan.org/tex-archive/macros/latex/contrib/logicproof?lang=en)  is another package for setting proofs,  in which subproofs have boxes round them in the style of e.g. *Logic in Com­puter Science* by Huth and Ryan (Alan Davidson, 2014). See also [here](https://tex.stackexchange.com/questions/268079/fitch-style-predicate-logic-proof) for how to declare variables.

**Natural deduction, Lemmon-style**

- There is a package [nd3.sty](/resources/nd3.sty) for setting Lemmon-style proofs, with a [short manual](pdfs/nd-manual2a.pdf) (Alex Steinberg 2010: see [here](http://phloxgroup.wordpress.com/2010/03/22/lemmon-style-natural-deduction-proofs-in-latex/) for the original post about this).


Revised 4 June 2026.
